title: The One in Vegas (Part 2)
weakest_section: 16:00-17:00 range with fewer strong jokes during transition

jokes.text: Let me guess. Um, Pancho Villa.
jokes.setup: Phoebe sees Ross with facial hair drawn on
jokes.punchline: She misidentifies it as a Pancho Villa costume
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe misinterpretation that reveals her unique worldview. Well-integrated character comedy.

jokes.text: And you're Bob Saget.
jokes.setup: After identifying Ross as Pancho Villa, looks at Rachel
jokes.punchline: Identifies Rachel's facial hair as Bob Saget
jokes.explanation: Follow-up joke that escalates the absurdity. Bob Saget reference is oddly specific and funny.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! You drew on me?
jokes.setup: Rachel realizes what Phoebe meant about costumes
jokes.punchline: Her horrified realization she's been walking around with drawn-on facial hair
jokes.explanation: Good setup payoff structure, though the premise itself isn't highly original.

jokes.text: Whoa, what kind of party was this?
jokes.setup: Hearing that Ross drew on Rachel and she wet his pants
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's innocent question about what kind of party involves such activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe-style innocent misunderstanding. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Rach, wait. The men's room is that way.
jokes.setup: Rachel storms off with drawn-on facial hair
jokes.punchline: Ross points out she looks like she belongs in the men's room
jokes.explanation: Solid visual callback to her appearance, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Mole people?
jokes.setup: Monica mentions 'those people' referring to casino lurkers
jokes.punchline: Joey's mind goes to underground mole people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey non-sequitur. Shows his unique thought process with excellent economy.

jokes.text: Kills you?
jokes.setup: Monica explains lurkers swoop in after you give up
jokes.punchline: Joey assumes 'swoop in' means murder
jokes.explanation: Another great Joey misunderstanding that escalates the stakes absurdly.

jokes.text: That's how she paid for my dance... Karate lessons.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions her nana paid for dance lessons through gambling
jokes.punchline: Self-corrects to 'karate lessons' revealing family embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's tendency to cover embarrassing family details. Decent character work.

jokes.text: Dance karate? Yes, it's a deadly... but beautiful sport.
jokes.setup: Joey questions 'dance karate' combination
jokes.punchline: Monica doubles down with poetic description of nonexistent martial art
jokes.explanation: Monica's commitment to the lie creates a funny absurd concept. Good escalation.

jokes.text: I took it off, and then I drew it back on.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if the facial hair will come off
jokes.punchline: Ross reveals he removed it then redrew it, making things worse
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross making bad situation worse, classic character flaw. Good cringe factor.

jokes.text: Who's your friend? He's hot. Thanks, man.
jokes.setup: Chandler introduces Joey's 'hand twin'
jokes.punchline: Hand twin casually accepts compliment about being hot
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag about the absurdity of the hand twin concept.

jokes.text: None of you will live with me in my hand-shaped mansion. Except, uh, you. You can live in the thumb.
jokes.setup: Friends don't believe in his hand twin money-making scheme
jokes.punchline: Joey describes elaborate hand-mansion with thumb residence for Chandler
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey absurdist fantasy with perfect character integration. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, come on. I was there. I know he's the love of your life.
jokes.setup: Monica claims Richard means nothing to her
jokes.punchline: Chandler calls out the obvious lie based on what he witnessed
jokes.explanation: Solid relationship humor but fairly predictable. Gets the job done.

jokes.text: I wanted to make a dramatic scene, but I hate packing.
jokes.setup: Chandler with an empty suitcase trying to leave dramatically
jokes.punchline: Admits his dramatic exit failed because he's too lazy to pack
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler self-awareness and laziness. Great subversion of dramatic gesture trope.

jokes.text: Tall, dark hair, hand looks exactly like this?
jokes.setup: Joey looking for his hand twin
jokes.punchline: Describes person by hand appearance as if it's a normal identifying feature
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Joey's single-minded obsession with hands. Absurd but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Um, he said he thought I was funny.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks what else the hotel clerk said about the permanent marker
jokes.punchline: Ross deflects with irrelevant personal validation from the clerk
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's need for validation even in crisis. Good character moment with nice timing.

jokes.text: Ross, I am a human doodle!
jokes.setup: Ross tries to minimize Rachel's facial hair situation
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic self-description as 'human doodle'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, memorable phrase that perfectly captures the absurdity. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: There are tons of freaks here. No other. No.
jokes.setup: Ross realizes he just called Rachel a freak
jokes.punchline: His panicked attempt to walk back the insult makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross foot-in-mouth moment with good cringe factor.

jokes.text: There was some staring and pointing.
jokes.setup: Ross assured Rachel no one would notice her facial hair
jokes.punchline: His deadpan admission that people definitely noticed
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect deadpan delivery. Shows Ross was completely wrong.

jokes.text: Nope.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Ross really likes expensive macadamia nuts
jokes.punchline: Ross's flat denial while continuing to eat them
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective physical comedy contradiction. Great economy.

jokes.text: Get out of here, you lurker! Go on, get!
jokes.setup: Phoebe learned about casino lurkers from Monica
jokes.punchline: She aggressively chases away a suspected lurker like shooing an animal
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Phoebe's characteristic overcommitment to new concepts.

jokes.text: She couldn't live without the 'Chand love.'
jokes.setup: Monica explains why she couldn't stay mad at Chandler
jokes.punchline: Creates cutesy portmanteau 'Chand love' for Chandler's love
jokes.explanation: Fairly weak wordplay that feels forced. Shows Monica's relationship enthusiasm but not clever.

jokes.text: This hand is your hand, This hand is my hand, Oh, wait, that's your hand, No, wait, it's my hand
jokes.setup: Joey wrote a song for him and his hand twin
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'This Land is Your Land' but about hands, gets confused about which hand is which
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey absurdist creativity. Shows his commitment to terrible ideas with genuine enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I look like my great-aunt, Muriel.
jokes.setup: Rachel looking at herself with the drawn-on facial hair
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to elderly male relative
jokes.explanation: Decent visual comedy reference, though fairly standard 'look like elderly relative' joke.

jokes.text: Which is what we play to at this casino!
jokes.setup: Ross busts at 23 in blackjack
jokes.punchline: Desperately tries to pretend 23 is the winning number at this casino
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's desperate attempt to save face. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: A new pair of shoes for the Chan-Chan man!
jokes.setup: Chandler is on a winning streak gambling
jokes.punchline: His goofy celebration with ridiculous nickname for himself
jokes.explanation: Shows Chandler getting caught up in gambling excitement. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Pick a number! That is your only job.
jokes.setup: Monica hesitating about which number to call
jokes.punchline: Chandler's frustrated direction about her simple role in his gambling
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic showing gambling stress. Chandler's controlling nature emerges under pressure.

jokes.text: Thank you, Mr. Drunken Gambler.
jokes.setup: Random gambler gives Chandler relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry acknowledgment of the source
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan observation about taking advice from random drunk people. Good economy.

jokes.text: Because I had lunch with Rich... Me neither!
jokes.setup: Chandler says he can't remember why they fought
jokes.punchline: Monica starts to explain then gets caught up in the excitement and agrees she forgot too
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing how gambling excitement overrides relationship drama. Well-structured.

jokes.text: You roll another hard eight... and we get married here, tonight.
jokes.setup: Chandler caught up in gambling excitement with Monica
jokes.punchline: Stakes escalate to marriage proposal based on dice roll
jokes.explanation: Major plot turn that feels earned through escalating gambling excitement. High stakes comedy.

jokes.text: Shut up!
jokes.setup: Chandler proposes marriage based on dice roll
jokes.punchline: Monica's shocked but excited reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction. Perfect economy, shows Monica's excitement overriding shock.

jokes.text: It just got interesting!
jokes.setup: Gambling crowd hears about marriage proposal bet
jokes.punchline: Collective recognition that stakes just escalated dramatically
jokes.explanation: Good crowd reaction that heightens the moment's significance. Functions well as escalation.

jokes.text: You look that way, I look this way.
jokes.setup: Dice roll lands under the table for marriage bet
jokes.punchline: Monica's systematic approach to finding the crucial die
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's organized approach even in crisis. Good character moment with decent tension.

jokes.text: It's your call.
jokes.setup: Die is borderline between 4 and 5, determining if Chandler and Monica marry
jokes.punchline: Putting life-changing decision in couple's hands on ambiguous dice roll
jokes.explanation: Perfect tension moment that maximizes the stakes. Well-crafted suspense.

jokes.text: I wouldn't want you to lose a chunk of that pretty blond hair!
jokes.setup: Elderly woman confronting Phoebe about lurking
jokes.punchline: Threatening Phoebe while calling her hair pretty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of lurker conflict with backhanded compliment threat.

jokes.text: And then I'll be on your ass every hour of every day... till Monday... because that's when I go home.
jokes.setup: Phoebe threatens to follow the elderly woman everywhere
jokes.punchline: Dramatic threat undermined by vacation schedule limitation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that perfectly deflates dramatic tension with practical reality. Classic Phoebe logic.

jokes.text: What time? Maybe we can share a cab.
jokes.setup: Both women realize they leave Monday
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman suggests sharing transportation with her enemy
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the confrontation. Shows practical elderly person logic overriding conflict.

jokes.text: Oh, I forgot to dial.
jokes.setup: Rachel calls for room service from hotel room
jokes.punchline: Realizes she forgot to actually dial the phone
jokes.explanation: Simple drunk comedy mistake. Gets the job done but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Joey lives with a duck.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel are drunk and expressing love for Joey
jokes.punchline: Ross's random factual observation about Joey's living situation
jokes.explanation: Good drunk logic callback. Shows Ross's tendency to state random facts when emotional.

jokes.text: Sir, you've got a little something...
jokes.setup: Ross greeting hotel staff while still having drawn-on facial hair
jokes.punchline: Staff member politely trying to point out his appearance problem
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag callback. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Sells drugs to kids.
jokes.setup: Security asking if Phoebe's accusation about the elderly woman is true
jokes.punchline: Phoebe makes up serious criminal allegation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe escalation that goes way too far. Shows her willingness to say anything to win.

jokes.text: I won't go back to that hellhole!
jokes.setup: Security says they're just taking her outside
jokes.punchline: Woman assumes she's being arrested and reveals criminal past
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals unexpected criminal history. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: I have a condom in my wallet that I've had since I was 12.
jokes.setup: Need something old for wedding tradition
jokes.punchline: Ross reveals embarrassingly ancient condom
jokes.explanation: Good Ross overshare that reveals his romantic inexperience. Character-appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: We'll bring it back. Put it under your dress.
jokes.setup: Monica says taking hotel property is stealing
jokes.punchline: Ross's casual attitude about theft combined with hiding evidence
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's poor decision-making under pressure. Decent escalation of wedding desperation.

jokes.text: Hello. My name is Regina Phalange.
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting kicked out and returning in disguise
jokes.punchline: Uses her classic fake name Regina Phalange
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe callback to her recurring fake identity. High character integration and fan recognition.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. May I just say that you gentlemen have the exact same hands?
jokes.setup: Phoebe in disguise approaches Joey and his hand twin
jokes.punchline: Pretends to notice hand similarity as neutral observer
jokes.explanation: Good callback combining hand twin obsession with Regina Phalange identity. Helps Joey's cause.

jokes.text: If you leave now, I will chop off my hand and give it to you!
jokes.setup: Hand twin getting frustrated with Joey's persistence
jokes.punchline: Offers to literally give Joey his hand to make him go away
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that takes hand obsession to logical extreme. Dark but very funny.

jokes.text: I'm your hand twin!
jokes.setup: Joey being dragged away by security
jokes.punchline: Final desperate appeal to hand twin connection
jokes.explanation: Good culmination of Joey's obsession. Shows his commitment to terrible ideas even when failing.

jokes.text: No, it's the graduation song.
jokes.setup: Ross plays 'Wedding March' asking if it freaks Monica out
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact correction about the song's other use
jokes.explanation: Good Monica factual correction that deflates romantic moment. Shows her practical nature.

jokes.text: Hello, Mrs. Ross! Well, hello, Mr. Rachel!
jokes.setup: Ross and Monica about to get married while drunk
jokes.punchline: They practice married names but mix up gender conventions
jokes.explanation: Decent drunk logic wordplay that shows their confused state. Sets up major plot development.

tmdb_overview: In Vegas, Ross and Rachel's drunken night leads to an unexpected decision; Chandler and Monica reconsider their hasty plans; Phoebe and Joey face their own complications.
